author: cskiraly

author: cskiraly
content:
Some clients have issues handling both Discv4 and Discv5 on the same UDP port (<@&836981603216654336>, maybe also <@&1070471163219824730> ?). The issue with this is that you would have to switch version at one point, and that will break communications between your own versions. 

In Geth we have a simple fall-through design: get packet, if it's not v4, pass it on to next handler, which happens to be v5.

You can find the gist of it here for reference: https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/blob/918d46b942b9d9f2a13c02cf12a651ba9ccae879/p2p/discover/v4_udp.go#L555-L563

author: emmajamiesonhoare_30642
content:
yeah i think this is fine for reth. reth already has shared-port support, so setting `--discovery.v5.port=30303` doesn’t make it bind two separate sockets.

it triggers the shared socket path: discv5 owns the socket and anything it doesn’t recognize gets forwarded into discv4, basically the same fall-through idea as geth but the other way around.

the hive failure was just testing discv5 on `30303` while reth was listening on `9200`.
